scenes.title: Agent Interview Arena
scenes.subtitle: Measure how well people steer agents through real tasks.
scenes.narration: Agent Interview Arena is a LeetCode-style interview room for AI-assisted work.

scenes.title: The new interview loop
scenes.subtitle: The model matters. The operator loop matters more.
scenes.narration: The candidate reads a locked task, asks allowed clarifying questions, prompts the agent, produces artifacts, and submits the transcript as a pull request.

scenes.title: What gets measured
scenes.subtitle: Completion, quality, cost, and judgment.
scenes.narration: Each attempt records time, token buckets, estimated dollars, tool calls, model, output quality, prompt quality, and redaction flags.

scenes.title: PRs become submissions
scenes.subtitle: No backend. No database. GitHub is the audit trail.
scenes.narration: The plugin writes one submission JSON file, opens a GitHub pull request, and GitHub Actions runs deterministic evaluation.

scenes.title: Better company interviews
scenes.subtitle: Make AI use visible instead of pretending it is not there.
scenes.narration: Companies can test the real skill: how someone collaborates with an agent, recovers from mistakes, protects data, and ships useful artifacts.

scenes.title: Start with small apps
scenes.subtitle: Short app specs become the task bank.
scenes.narration: Small app ideas like markdown previewers, pomodoro timers, CSV converters, and password generators become locked arena challenges.
